items.title: Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
Splunk patched an OS command injection in AI Toolkit, while Atlassian fixed dozens of flaws in third-party dependencies.
The post Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Rokarolla Banking Trojan Targets 200 Applications
items.summary:
The Android malware allows its operators to take control of infected devices and harvest sensitive information.
The post Rokarolla Banking Trojan Targets 200 Applications appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE
items.summary:
Insufficient validation of user input allows an attacker to gain access to the underlying OS and elevate their privileges to root.
The post Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft fixes Windows Server 2016 security update failures
items.summary: Microsoft has fixed a known issue causing the June 2026 security updates to fail on Windows Server 2016 systems that weren't up to date. [...]

items.title: Scripting the disassembler: Local agentic reverse engineering through vbdec’s live COM object model
items.summary: Cisco Talos detailed a new approach to reverse engineering that pairs local AI agents with traditional analysis tools like the VB6 disassembler vbdec. Instead of awkwardly bolting AI onto the software, vbdec exposes its parsed data through a live COM interface.

items.title: F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
Critical flaws in NGINX could allow remote, unauthenticated attackers to cause a restart and potentially execute arbitrary code.
The post F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal
items.summary:
Israel-based Entro specializes in non-human identity and credential security solutions, and it will enable SailPoint to enhance its products.
The post SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Kodak Admits Data Breach After ShinyHunters Hack Claims
items.summary:
Kodak told SecurityWeek it believes there is no threat to its systems or operations as a result of the cybersecurity incident.
The post Kodak Admits Data Breach After ShinyHunters Hack Claims appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: EU Gets a Head Start in Developing 6G Network Security
items.summary: "Shield-6G" will combine AI threat detection, digital twins, honeypots, and more, to help carriers protect 6G networks against the threats of tomorrow.

items.title: Choice, compliance, and collaboration: Europe’s path to open digital sovereignty
items.summary:
The European Commission’s Tech Sovereignty Package comes at a defining moment for the continent's digital future. European competitiveness and security are top of the agenda for European business, institutions, and citizens, and a significant investment in European digital capacity is needed to deliver those goals. In that context, it is understandable that Europe is considering how to boost the European Union digital footprint from chips, to cloud adoption, to AI data infrastructure.
The European Commission’s strategy is to be grounded in "openness, partnership, and fair competition." Indeed,

items.title: Midjourney goes from generating cat images to full-body ultrasound scans
items.summary: Midjourney CEO David Holz just showed off the company's first hardware product and plans to build a San Francisco spa, which he admitted is a bit different from the "cat pictures" produced by its AI image generator. Dubbed The Midjourney Scanner, it's an ultrasound-based full-body scanner that uses a ring of sensors to capture vertical […]

items.title: Leak confirms OpenAI is testing a ChatGPT for Science subscription
items.summary: OpenAI appears to be testing a new subscription and experience for science use cases, but it's unclear if it'll be available to everyone regardless of their background. [...]

items.title: How to turn off AI in your Google Docs
items.summary: Here's what you need to do to get those pesky "write with Gemini" pop-ups to go away.

items.title: GLM-5.2 is probably the most powerful text-only open weights LLM
items.summary:
Chinese AI lab Z.ai released GLM-5.2 to their coding plan subscribers on June 13th, and then yesterday (June 16th) released the full open weights under an MIT license. Similar in size to their previous GLM-5 and GLM-5.1 releases, this is 753B parameter, 1.51TB monster - with 40 active parameters (Mixture of Experts). GLM-5.2 is a text input only model - Z.ai have a separate vision family most recently represented by GLM-5V-Turbo, but that one isn't open weights. GLM-5.2 has a 1 million token context window, up from GLM-5.1's 200,000.
The buzz around this model is strong.
Artificial Analysis, w
